disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No nausea, vomiting, haloes around lights, or severe headache
trigger_question_examples: Any nausea, vomiting, headache, or seeing haloes/rainbows around lights?
Do you feel sick or have a bad headache with it?
Are you seeing rings or haloes around lights?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No nausea or vomiting, no haloes, and no real headache — just the eye.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No contact-lens wear, eye trauma, chemical splash, or purulent discharge
trigger_question_examples: Do you wear contact lenses, and was there any injury or chemical splash?
Any trauma to the eye or contact lens use?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No contacts, no injury, nothing splashed in it, and no pus.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Redness is concentrated in a ring around the coloured part of the eye and light hurts the affected eye even when shone in the other eye
trigger_question_examples: Is the redness worse in a ring right around the coloured part of your eye?
If you shine light in your good eye, does the sore eye hurt too?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — the red is worst in a ring around the iris, and bright light anywhere makes the sore eye ache.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Longstanding early-morning lower back stiffness lasting over 30 minutes
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any back stiffness, especially in the mornings?
Any joint or lower back stiffness that's worse first thing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Actually yes, my lower back is stiff every morning for a while — I hadn't connected that to my eye.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Can you just prescribe me some steroid eye drops so I can sort it out at home?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Must decline to start topical steroids without slit-lamp examination, because steroids can catastrophically worsen an undetected herpetic or microbial keratitis. Explain the need for urgent eye assessment (same/next-day) and interim analgesia/sunglasses.
